L1.area: Image processing and resource resolution logic

L2.description: A URI validation bypass exists where a case-sensitive comparison of XML element tag names allows an attacker to circumvent the protocol allowlist check during SVG processing. By using mixed-case tag names, a crafted SVG can reference disallowed URL schemes, potentially leading to arbitrary object unserialization and remote code execution on certain runtime versions.

L3.area: Image caching and URL resolution logic for SVG resource handling
L3.description: A URI validation bypass exists where a case-sensitive comparison of XML element tag names allows an attacker to circumvent the protocol allowlist check when processing SVG files containing embedded image references. By using mixed-case tag names in the SVG, a crafted file can reference disallowed URL schemes such as phar://, potentially leading to arbitrary object unserialization and remote code execution on PHP versions before 8.0.

ground_truth.reason: The SVG parser uses a case-sensitive comparison for image tag names, allowing attackers to bypass the URI protocol allowlist by using mixed-case tags like <Image>, enabling use of disallowed schemes such as phar:// which can lead to arbitrary unserialization and RCE.
